_patch_meta.produced_by: Claude AI — Registry Mode Phase C
_patch_meta.description: Dimension assignments for 4 new H7965G shalom groups (2508-001/002/003/004, vc_group_id 3581-3584). Created from H7965 shalom reinstatement 2026-05-01. No group description corrections. One SD pointer inserted. Registry stamp included.

set.notes: Shalom as the positive inner experiential condition of the whole person — peace, rest, welfare, and wholeness inwardly. The God-oriented soul's condition when shalom is present. Lam 3:17 (soul bereft of peace) confirms the characteristic by showing its absence in the nephesh. Isa 26:3 (perfect peace for the trusting mind) and Psa 4:8 (in peace I will lie down and sleep) are the primary positive expressions. Human is the dominant bearer; God is the source/ground but the condition itself is the human experiential state.

operations.description: Dimension assignment for 2508-001: shalom as inner condition — Emotion–Positive, HUMAN

set.notes: Shalom as God's covenantal inner orientation toward his people — expressed as giving (Num 6:26), speaking (Psa 85:8), and eternally confirming (Isa 54:10) peace. God consistently initiates and sustains; the human is the recipient. Withdrawal (Jer 16:5) is the negative expression of the same relational characterisation. Distinct from group 2508-004: this group is about God's covenantal disposition, not about the peace embodied in the messianic person.

operations.description: Dimension assignment for 2508-002: shalom as covenantal peace — Relational Disposition, GOD

set.notes: Shalom as the stable inner orientation of the human person toward peace — as identity (Psa 120:7: I am for peace), character (Mal 2:6: walked in peace and uprightness), and moral imperative (Psa 34:14: seek peace and pursue it). The group includes authentic peace-orientation and its false simulation (prophets crying Peace when there is no peace) — both confirm that shalom-as-orientation is a genuine moral-character quality capable of authentic and counterfeit instantiation. Moral Character preferred over Volition (stable orientation vs. discrete acts) and Relational Disposition (inner quality of person vs. orientation toward a specific other).

operations.description: Dimension assignment for 2508-003: shalom as inner orientation toward peace — Moral Character, HUMAN

set.notes: Shalom as the peace constitutively identified with and proceeding from the messianic king — who is both divinely appointed (Prince of Peace, Isa 9:6) and humanly embodied (He shall be their peace, Mic 5:5; chastisement purchased through suffering, Isa 53:5). Peace here is not God giving a condition to humans (2508-002) nor the human inner state received (2508-001) — it is the characteristic that is ontologically identified with the person who bridges the divine-human boundary. DHC assigned: the divine and human poles are equally constitutive. Dominant_subject = NONE: the dual nature of the messianic subject cannot be resolved to one pole. Strong SD003 cross-registry connection to R117 Greek groups (Eph 2:14 — he himself is our peace) and R130 (reconciliation, Col 1:20).

operations.description: Dimension assignment for 2508-004: shalom as messianic peace — Divine-Human Correspondence, NONE

record.description: Group 2508-004 (H7965G sha.lom — messianic/eschatological peace) establishes that shalom in the Hebrew prophetic corpus is constitutively identified with the person of the messianic king: Prince of Peace (Isa 9:6), He shall be their peace (Mic 5:5), upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace (Isa 53:5). This is the OT ground of the NT claim that Christ IS peace (Eph 2:14, G1515 eirene — R117 group 426-003). Session D must examine whether the he-shall-be-their-peace structure (person = peace) in the Hebrew prophetic corpus is the direct semantic antecedent of he-himself-is-our-peace in the NT — and whether this constitutes a single unified concept across both testaments that R117 shalom and eirene groups together document. Cross-registry targets: NT eirene registries (R117 own Greek groups), R130 reconciliation (Col 1:20), messianic-identity material.

operations.description: Insert SD pointer DIM-117-SD003: messianic peace (OT) as antecedent of Christ IS peace (NT)
Registry stamp: R117 peace dim_review_status = Complete
